records.summary: ExpressionParser is the public non-owning parser facade over completed-input, iterator, interpolation, and custom expression result families.
records.rationale: The parser facade should remain parser machinery rather than a kernel materializer. Callers decide value ownership and wrap parser publications in products only when the publication becomes semantically useful.
guidance: Start here when a public parse entry family, base-span context, or parser result algebra boundary changes.
Keep parser publication non-owning; template/compiler/value-site layers decide product ownership.
Do not add another adapter layer above this facade just to smooth over a feature-local parse case.

anchors.summary: Expression parser package intent and non-owning parser boundary.

records.summary: SemanticRuntime and SemanticApp are intentionally the in-process boot/open-app facade, not a second materialization layer.
records.rationale: The API README explicitly keeps runtime.ts as the boot/app facade while pushing public contracts, row projections, and query-family answerers into focused modules. Future authoring or LSP expansion should preserve that boundary rather than letting runtime.ts become another private summary table.
guidance: Use SemanticRuntime for workspace boot, app opening, project selection, cursor-locus convenience methods, and app-world cache policy.
Use SemanticApp for public query dispatch and compact app-level query methods; move substrate-family answerers into focused modules when the facade starts owning projection logic.

Keep `SemanticRuntime.authoringCatalog()` / `AuthoringCatalog` as the static ontology/recipe-contract projection and `AuthoringOrientation` as the opened-app evidence projection; callers should not need to spelunk ontology.ts or infer catalog contracts from an arbitrary app.
When an answer feels awkward, improve underlying product records or add a narrow query projection before adding compatibility glue to runtime.ts.

anchors.summary: Static authoring ontology and recipe contract projection.
Template-family query boundary for template rows, completions, cursor info, and template diagnostics.

anchors.heading: Semantic Runtime API
anchors.summary: API boundary intent and split guidance.

records.summary: Semantic-runtime diagnostics have an app-level aggregation query; use this as domain guidance when diagnostic work is touched, not as a default autonomous frontier while framework-error gaps are closed.
records.rationale: Template diagnostics cover parser, compiler, binding data-flow, and runtime AST expression-call pressure; resource convergence contributes bindable/process-content/watch/controller watcher issue products; configuration contributes source/API and framework-service issue products; and DI contributes container/resource/ambient-resolve issue products. Atlas framework.errors currently reports no active mapped diagnostic gaps, so this record should steer future diagnostic work only when a new exact framework-error or authoring-policy pressure appears. Product-owned issue records should remain in the substrate that owns the framework behavior, then aggregate through AppDiagnostics.
guidance: This frontier is nextActionPolicy=when-touched: do not choose it as an unfiltered autonomous next task while `pressure:framework-errors` continues to report zero active mapped diagnostic gaps.

Start here when a framework error frontier points at non-template source such as @watch, bindable metadata, DI resolver calls, router config, AppTask/customization callbacks, observer service configuration, or package configuration.

Do not attach framework-error-code authority at the API boundary. Add or reuse a product-owned issue/diagnostic record in the substrate that owns the framework behavior, then let the API aggregate it.

Keep template diagnostics as one diagnostic source, not the only diagnostics contract. AppDiagnostics currently aggregates configuration, DI, template, resource, router, and route-recognizer diagnostic products by source locus while preserving diagnosticDomain and relatedQueryKind.
When aggregating diagnostics, collect unpaged rows from each owning diagnostic family before applying the app-level page. Child-query paging before aggregation creates misleading pressure counts.
Use Atlas framework.errors diagnostic frontiers and likelySemanticRuntimeOwner hints to choose the owning substrate, then inspect exact framework usage rows before adding framework-code links.

For broad fixture sweeps, use compact app-pressure fixture-owner rows (`templates.diagnostic-error-code-fixtures`, `app.diagnostic-error-code-fixtures`, `bindings.data-flow-error-code-fixtures`, `bindings.target-access-error-code-fixtures`, and `router.error-code-fixtures`) before doing isolated runs. These rows point exact framework codes at public fixture keys while custom/external roots collapse to `custom-root`.

Treat runtime-html ast* as partial and binding-utils-owned: AUR0103 missing value converter, AUR0101 missing binding behavior, and AUR0102 duplicate authored binding behavior are template diagnostic rows with resource-registration or expression-rewrite suggestions; other runtime-html ast labels are dormant until exact framework usage and semantic owner are modeled.

Treat runtime-html node* as partial across configuration, observation, and controller ownership. AUR0653 is claimed for duplicate NodeObserverLocator mappings through ConfigurationIssue; AUR0652 is claimed through target-access/template diagnostics only for the exact dirty-check-disabled observer lookup path; host-node controller codes still need controller topology semantics before they can be claimed.

Treat runtime Scope static API nullish guards as configuration source/API diagnostics: AUR0203/AUR0204 are ConfigurationIssue rows from direct Scope.fromParent/getContext/create calls, not rendered template lookup misses.

Treat runtime-html invalid* as partial: AUR0227/AUR0229 bindable decorator errors and AUR0766 processContent hook errors are ResourceIssue products, while runtime computed_not_getter / AUR0228 is an ObservationIssue from source-backed @computed(...) call-form decorators on non-getter/non-method targets. AUR0769/AUR0771 need app-root platform host state or Aurelia instance lifecycle modeling before they can be claimed.

Treat runtime-html compiler_attr_mapper_duplicate_mapping / AUR0719 as configuration-service pressure: AppTask-time AttrMapper duplicate useMapping/useGlobalMapping calls surface through ConfigurationIssue and AppDiagnostics under the configuration domain.

Treat runtime-html watch* as complete through ResourceIssue products. Treat runtime-html controller* as split by owning framework path: AUR0501 is a resource-definition issue, AUR0506 is a resource watcher issue, and AUR0507/AUR0508 are runtime controller observer-setup issues. Lifecycle-state controller errors still need controller-state emulation before they can be claimed.

Treat runtime-html au* as partial and AuCompose-owned: AUR0805/AUR0809 are static setter diagnostics through RuntimeControllerIssue when lowered bindable SetPropertyInstruction values are invalid; AUR0806 is a static string component/view-model lookup diagnostic against the parent hydration-context container; composition controller run/deactivate errors still need lifecycle modeling before they can be claimed.

Runtime-html branch link-hook diagnostics are RuntimeControllerIssue rows: AUR0810 for else without a previous if sibling, AUR0815 for case/default-case without a parent switch controller, and AUR0816 for duplicate default-case controllers under one switch.

Runtime-html promise-result link-hook diagnostics are RuntimeControllerIssue rows: AUR0813 for pending/then/catch controllers that are not rendered under the synthetic view of a parent promise.resolve controller.

Runtime-html renderer-dispatch diagnostics are RuntimeRendererIssue rows when no binding/controller product exists yet. As of 2026-05-13 RefBindingRenderer view.ref maps to not_supported_view_ref_api / AUR0750, custom-element-host named misses map to ref_not_found / AUR0751 after same-node custom attribute targets are checked, controller/component refs on ordinary elements map to node_is_not_a_host / AUR0762, named custom-element fallback on ordinary elements maps to node_is_not_a_host2 / AUR0763, and SpreadValueRenderer invalid targets map to spreading_invalid_target / AUR0820 through runtime-renderer-framework-error template/app diagnostics.

Runtime-html IViewFactory diagnostics are controller-activation pressure, not generic DI. Ordinary custom elements/custom attributes that resolve IViewFactory during instance activation map to view_factory_provider_not_ready / AUR0755 through RuntimeControllerIssue; template controllers receive the prepared provider and should not be diagnosed.

Runtime-html portal static activation diagnostics are RuntimeControllerIssue rows over inline multi-binding props: AUR0779 for unsupported literal InsertPosition, AUR0811 for strict empty target, and AUR0812 for strict missing target. The API should surface these as framework-code template/app diagnostics only after the compiler substrate has lowered the portal attribute's secondary grammar; neighboring HTML attributes are not portal bindable props.

anchors.summary: App-level diagnostic aggregation over owning diagnostic products.
Template diagnostic projection for template-owned parser/compiler/binding issues.
Configuration issue projection for configuration-owned framework diagnostics.
Resource issue projection for resource-owned framework diagnostics.
DI issue projection for DI-owned framework diagnostics.
Public query enum exposing TemplateDiagnostics, ResourceIssues, DiIssues, and AppDiagnostics.
Framework error-code frontiers with exact semantic-runtime coverage and likely owner hints.

records.summary: Project-shape triage is the formal app/resource-library/package policy for openApp, LSP-style project selection, and workspace-child package context.
records.rationale: External monorepos can contain real Aurelia apps, resource libraries, Aurelia-adjacent packages, and non-Aurelia packages. Boot must cheaply classify those frames from local manifest dependencies, ancestor workspace manifests that explicitly include child packages, source roles, and Aurelia facade activation/source signals, while app-world construction must fail closed when no app-shaped default exists instead of opening an arbitrary app-source package as a fake app.
guidance: Use SemanticProjectShapeKind and SemanticProjectAnalysisKind before adding another app-like heuristic.

Carry SemanticProjectAureliaDependencyOrigin and SemanticProjectShapeReasonCount through summaries when monorepo selection feels surprising; do not flatten workspace-context evidence into an unlabeled dependency count.

Keep Atlas workspace.architecture aligned with this policy: it should surface workspace-manifest dependency mechanisms and bounded file-inventory source signals for package frames whose tsconfig omits app source.
Default openApp() should choose only an aurelia-app project; resource-library authoring must be selected explicitly by projectKey or sourceFilePath.

Keep project-shape detection in boot/admission: source roles and manifest/workspace membership are allowed there, but do not recognize resources, routes, DI, templates, or configuration products there.
Pressure scripts may select aurelia-resource-library frames for authoring/LSP pressure, but missing app roots in those frames are not app-startup failures.

anchors.summary: Default app-opening policy: pick aurelia-app or fail closed.
Boot boundary and project-shape triage notes.
API project selection and monorepo pressure policy.
